title: Miso Ramen
description: A deeply soul-satisfying bowl of Japanese ramen. This version features a complex, nutty miso broth, chewy hand-folded noodles, and a perfectly jammy ajitsuke tamago. The secret lies in the 'tare'—the flavor base that gives miso ramen its signature depth.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Pork bones or Chicken carcass","qty":"1","unit":"kg","notes":"for the base broth"},{"name":"Red & White Miso Paste","qty":"4","unit":"tbsp","notes":"for a balanced tare"},{"name":"Ramen Noodles","qty":"200","unit":"g","notes":"fresh alkaline noodles"},{"name":"Pork Belly (Chashu)","qty":"200","unit":"g","notes":"braised and sliced"},{"name":"Ajitsuke Tamago","qty":"1","unit":"pc","notes":"soft-boiled marinated egg"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Long Broth**: Simmer the bones with aromatics (ginger, garlic, scallions) for at least 6 hours (or 45 mins in a pressure cooker) until the liquid is creamy and opaque. Strain and keep hot.
2. **The Miso Tare**: In a small pan, whisk miso pastes with a splash of mirin, soy sauce, and a hint of sesame oil. Warm gently to marry the flavors without boiling the miso.
3. **Assemble the Bowl**: Place 2 tablespoons of tare in a deep pre-warmed bowl. Ladle in 300ml of boiling broth and whisk to incorporate. Add boiled noodles, then top with chashu, the jammy egg, and bamboo shoots.
cookwareJson: ["Large stock pot or pressure cooker", "Whisk", "Deep ramen bowls", "Noodle strainer"]

title: Sushi Platter (Chef's Selection)
description: A refined collection of Nigiri and Maki, emphasizing the purity of fresh, sustainable seafood and the perfect balance of seasoned vinegared rice. This isn't just a meal; it's an exercise in temperature and texture control.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Sushi Grade Tuna/Salmon","qty":"300","unit":"g","notes":"ultra-fresh"},{"name":"Koshihikari Rice","qty":"2","unit":"cups","notes":"short-grain Japanese rice"},{"name":"Rice Vinegar","qty":"50","unit":"ml","notes":"seasoned with sugar and salt"},{"name":"Nori Sheets","qty":"5","unit":"pcs","notes":"high-quality toasted seaweed"}]
stepsMd:
1. **Perfect Shari (Rice)**: Wash rice 5-6 times until water is clear. Cook with slightly less water than usual. While hot, fold in the vinegar mixture using a 'slicing' motion to avoid mashing grains. Fan the rice to cool quickly for a glossy finish.
2. **Nigiri Technique**: Slice the fish at a 45-degree angle against the grain. Wet your hands with 'tezu' (vinegar water), form a small oblong of rice, and drape the fish over it, pressing gently to bond.
3. **Maki Rolling**: Place nori on a bamboo mat. Spread rice thinly, leaving 1cm at the top. Place fillings in the center and roll tightly, using the mat to create a perfect cylinder.
cookwareJson: ["Rice cooker", "Hangiri (wooden rice tub) or glass bowl", "Bamboo rolling mat", "Yanagiba (sashimi knife)"]

title: Okonomiyaki (Osaka Style)
description: Known as 'Japanese Savory Pancake', this Osaka-style comfort food is a hearty, customizable feast. Cabbage-heavy batter is grilled to a golden brown and topped with an umami-rich dance of bonito flakes and creamy Kewpie mayo.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Shredded Cabbage","qty":"4","unit":"cups","notes":"finely sliced"},{"name":"Flour & Dashi","qty":"1","unit":"cup","notes":"batter base"},{"name":"Nagaimo (Mountain Yam)","qty":"50","unit":"g","notes":"grated, for fluffiness"},{"name":"Pork Belly strips","qty":"100","unit":"g","notes":"thinly sliced"},{"name":"Okonomiyaki Sauce & Mayo","qty":"2","unit":"tbsp","notes":"for drizzling"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Batter**: Mix flour, dashi, and grated nagaimo into a smooth batter. Fold in the cabbage, green onions, and tempura bits until every strand is coated. Do not overmix.
2. **The Grill**: On a hot griddle or non-stick pan, form a thick disk of the mixture. Place pork belly strips on top. Cover and cook for 5 minutes until the base is crispy and golden.
3. **The Flip**: Carefully flip the pancake. Press down slightly and cook for another 5 minutes. Finish by brushing with thick sauce, drizzling mayo in a lattice pattern, and sprinkling a mountain of bonito flakes.
cookwareJson: ["Large flat griddle or non-stick skillet", "Two large spatulas (for flipping)", "Mixing bowl"]

title: Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Perhaps the world's most beloved curry. Succulent pieces of yogurt-marinated chicken are charred in a tandoor (or hot oven) and then simmered in a velvety, spice-rich tomato and cream sauce.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Chicken Thighs","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"cubed"},{"name":"Greek Yogurt","qty":"200","unit":"g","notes":"for marinade"},{"name":"Garam Masala","qty":"2","unit":"tsp","notes":"high quality"},{"name":"Heavy Cream","qty":"100","unit":"ml","notes":"for the velvet texture"},{"name":"Canned Tomatoes","qty":"400","unit":"g","notes":"crushed or pureed"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Char**: Marinate chicken in yogurt and spices for at least 2 hours. Broil at the highest oven setting or grill until the edges are blackened and charred. This smoke is essential for the 'tikka' flavor.
2. **The Gravy**: Sauté onions until golden, then add ginger-garlic paste and tomatoes. Cook until the oil separates from the masala. This 'bhuna' process is the secret to deep flavor.
3. **Finish**: Stir in the cream and the charred chicken. Simmer for 10 minutes. Garnish with a generous handful of fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lemon.
cookwareJson: ["Heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven", "Baking sheet or grill", "Wooden spoon"]

title: Palak Paneer
description: A vibrant, emerald-green North Indian classic. Fresh spinach is blanched and pureed into a silky sauce, enriched with warming spices and chunks of golden-seared paneer cheese.
stepsMd:
1. **Emerald Base**: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 2 minutes, then immediately shock in ice water to preserve the color. Puree with one green chili until smooth.
2. **The Tempering**: Fry cumin seeds in ghee until they splutter. Add onions and ginger-garlic paste. Cook until fragrant but not brown. Add the spinach puree and a touch of garam masala.
3. **The Finish**: Gently sear paneer cubes in a separate pan until golden. Fold them into the spinach gravy. Add a splash of cream and a pinch of kasuri methi (dried fenugreek) for that restaurant-style aroma.

title: Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)
description: The aristocrat of Indian curries. This dish is all about luxury: a smooth-as-silk tomato gravy, finished with an unholy amount of butter and cream. Originally created in Delhi, it has become a global obsession.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Smoked Chicken Tikka","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"shredded or cubed"},{"name":"Unsalted Butter","qty":"100","unit":"g","notes":"the star of the show"},{"name":"Tomato Puree","qty":"2","unit":"cups","notes":"strained for smoothness"},{"name":"Kasuri Methi","qty":"1","unit":"tbsp","notes":"crushed between palms"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Makhani Sauce**: Simmer tomato puree with butter, ginger, and honey. Reduce until thick and vibrant. Pass through a fine-mesh sieve if you want that true 'velvet' texture.
2. **The Infusion**: Add roasted chicken pieces to the sauce. Stir in cream and let it simmer on the lowest heat possible. The chicken should slowly absorb the buttery gravy.
3. **The Final Flourish**: Add more butter (yes, more) and crushed kasuri methi. The aroma should be intoxicatingly dairy-sweet and herbal. Serve with buttery garlic naan.

title: Spaghetti Carbonara
description: A masterclass in Italian minimalism. Just four ingredients: pasta, eggs, Pecorino Romano, and guanciale. No cream allowed—the silkiness comes purely from the emulsification of egg and starchy pasta water.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Spaghetti","qty":"200","unit":"g","notes":"high-quality bronze-die"},{"name":"Guanciale (or Pancetta)","qty":"100","unit":"g","notes":"sliced into batons"},{"name":"Pecorino Romano","qty":"50","unit":"g","notes":"freshly grated"},{"name":"Farm Eggs","qty":"2","unit":"pcs","notes":"plus 1 extra yolk"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Crisp**: Render the fat from the guanciale in a cold pan, slowly bringing it up to heat until the pork is crispy and the fat is golden and liquid. Turn off the heat.
2. **The Egg Base**: Whisk the eggs and Pecorino with a mountain of freshly cracked black pepper until it forms a thick paste.
3. **The Emulsion**: Boil pasta until barely al dente. Toss the hot pasta into the guanciale pan. While off the heat, pour in the egg-cheese mixture and a splash of pasta water. Toss vigorously until a creamy, glossy sauce coats every strand.

title: Beef Lasagna (Lasagne alla Bolognese)
description: The ultimate Sunday project. Layers of silky handmade egg pasta, a slow-simmered beef and pork ragù, and a nutmeg-scented béchamel sauce. This is the authentic version from Emilia-Romagna, not the 'cheese-heavy' variants.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Minced Beef & Pork","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"70/30 mix"},{"name":"Lasagna Sheets","qty":"12","unit":"pcs","notes":"egg-based fresh pasta"},{"name":"Whole Milk","qty":"1","unit":"L","notes":"for Béchamel"},{"name":"Parmigiano Reggiano","qty":"100","unit":"g","notes":"aged 24 months"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Ragù**: Sauté soffritto (onion, carrot, celery) in lard or butter. Brown the meats deeply. Deglaze with white wine and simmer with a tiny bit of tomato and milk for 3 hours.
2. **The Béchamel**: Make a roux with butter and flour. Slowly whisk in hot milk until you have a thick, luxurious sauce. Season with a generous amount of freshly grated nutmeg.
3. **The Assembly**: Layer ragù, béchamel, and pasta sheets in a deep tray. Repeat for 6 layers. Finish with a thick coating of Parmigiano. Bake until the edges are crispy and the center is bubbling and tender.
cookwareJson: ["Large heavy pot for ragù", "Saucepan for Béchamel", "Deep rectangular baking dish", "Whisk"]

title: Risotto ai Funghi (Mushroom Risotto)
description: A northern Italian treasure that requires patience and a rhythmic arm. Arborio rice is toasted and then slowly hydrated with rich stock until it reaches a state of 'all'onda'—wavy, creamy, and perfectly al dente.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Arborio or Carnaroli Rice","qty":"300","unit":"g","notes":"high-starch rice"},{"name":"Porcini & Cremini Mushrooms","qty":"400","unit":"g","notes":"dried porcini adds intense depth"},{"name":"Vegetable Stock","qty":"1.5","unit":"L","notes":"keep at a low simmer"},{"name":"Cold Butter & Parmesan","qty":"50","unit":"g","notes":"for mantecatura"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Toast**: Sauté shallots and mushrooms until the water has evaporated. Add the rice and toast until the edges are translucent—it should smell nutty. Deglaze with dry white wine.
2. **The Ritual**: Add stock one ladle at a time, stirring constantly. Wait for the liquid to be mostly absorbed before adding the next. This friction releases starch, creating the creaminess.
3. **Mantecatura**: When the rice is al dente, turn off the heat. Vigorously beat in the cold butter and Parmesan. Cover for 2 minutes and serve immediately—it should wait for no one.
cookwareJson: ["Wide, flat-bottomed pan (sauté pan)", "Stock pot", "Wooden spoon with a hole (traditional for risotto)"]

title: Tacos al Pastor
description: Mexico City's vertical-spit masterpiece. Pork marinated in achiote and chilies, thinly sliced and served with pineapple to cut through the richness. A sweet, savory, and spicy street-food icon.
ingredientsJson: [{"name":"Pork Shoulder","qty":"500","unit":"g","notes":"thinly sliced"},{"name":"Achiote Paste","qty":"50","unit":"g","notes":"for the signature red color"},{"name":"Guajillo Chilies","qty":"3","unit":"pcs","notes":"dried and rehydrated"},{"name":"Fresh Pineapple","qty":"1/2","unit":"pc","notes":"sliced for roasting"},{"name":"Corn Tortillas","qty":"12","unit":"pcs","notes":"warm and soft"}]
stepsMd:
1. **The Adobo**: Blend achiote, chilies, vinegar, and pineapple juice into a thick paste. Slather over the pork and marinate overnight. This tenderizes the meat through acidity.
2. **The Roast**: If you don't have a trompo, stack the pork slices tightly with a pineapple slice on top and roast vertically (using a skewer in an onion base) until charred and crispy.
3. **The Taco**: Shave the crispy pork and roast pineapple. Serve on double-stacked tortillas with finely diced onions, cilantro, and a squeeze of lime.
cookwareJson: ["Blender", "Vertical skewer setup or cast-iron skillet", "Tongs", "Warm tortilla holder"]
